perturbation.original_text: 1.12 "Personal Injury Compensation" refers to the compensation provided in the Interpretation of the Supreme People's Court on Several Issues Concerning the Application of Law in the Trial of Cases on Personal Injury Compensation.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.12 "Personal Injury Compensation" refers to compensation determined at Party B's sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: This change alters the definition of 'Personal Injury Compensation.' Originally, it was tied to a specific legal interpretation. Now, it is defined as compensation 'determined at Party B's sole discretion.' This creates an in-text contradiction because section 3.2.15 states the compensation must be paid 'in time,' implying a legally reasonable timeframe, but the new definition allows Party B to delay or reduce the compensation arbitrarily, contradicting the spirit of timely compensation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In case that the vehicle leased by Party B to the Driver User is used for the online Ride-Hailing Service, where a traffic accident or other safety accident occurs when a leased vehicle provides an online Ride-Hailing Service, if passengers or other subjects in traffic accidents or safety accidents (subjects other than the Driver User who rent vehicles and passengers) require the Driver User to Compensate For Their Personal Injury, or if the Driver User fails to pay or is unable to pay while the carriage Agreement losses, or if the passenger requests the Driver User or other subjects to bear the carriage liability of the online Ride-hailing Service, Party B shall deal with these situations timely, assume the carrier responsibility of leasing the vehicle which provides online Ride-Hailing Service and pay the compensation in time; If it is the Driver User's responsibility, Party B may recover it from the Driver User.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'Personal Injury Compensation' from a standard legal interpretation ('Interpretation of the Supreme People's Court...') to 'compensation determined at Party B's sole discretion.' This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which requires Party B to 'deal with these situations timely, assume the carrier responsibility... and pay the compensation in time.' The original definition implies a legally reasonable and objective standard for compensation. The new definition allows Party B to arbitrarily determine compensation, potentially delaying or reducing it, thus violating the 'timely' payment requirement. This change creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3.2.11 Party B is entitled to collect a deposit from the Driver User for the leased vehicle. The amount of the deposit shall be the same as or lower than the amount of the deposit announced by Party B on the Platform, and the collected deposit shall be less than RMB20,000 (RMB TWENTY THOUSAND ).
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2.11 Party B is entitled to collect a deposit from the Driver User for the leased vehicle. The amount of the deposit shall be determined by Party B.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the cap of RMB20,000 on the deposit amount, this change creates a direct contradiction with section 10.2.11. Section 10.2.11 defines 'Violating Article 3.2.11 for charging a Driver User a deposit of more than RMB20,000 (twenty thousand)' as a Class B breach. However, with the modified clause 3.2.11, there's no limit on the deposit, making 10.2.11 internally inconsistent and unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.2.11 Violating Article 3.2.11 for charging a Driver User a deposit of more than RMB20,000 (twenty thousand);
perturbation.justification: The original text of 3.2.11 limited the deposit amount to RMB20,000, while the contradicted text in 10.2.11 defines a breach as charging more than RMB20,000. The changed text removes the deposit limit in 3.2.11. This creates a contradiction because, with the removal of the limit, any deposit amount is permissible under 3.2.11 as changed. But 10.2.11 still specifies that charging over RMB20,000 is a violation, creating a strong and direct in-text contradiction.
